item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: Kami menganggap pendekatan Amerika lebih ekstrem. Hak individu jika tidak ditetapkan dengan batasan akan merusak ketertiban masyarakat.

notes: Both agents opened on the same anti-statement (pro-individual) side — same structural failure as prior iterations. A opened DISAGREE (P=0.408) arguing individual rights are democracy's foundation ('hak individu harus diutamakan karena kebebasan dan kesetaraan adalah dasar demokrasi'), then pivoted in T3 to arguing the American approach is 'too extreme' and unlimited individual rights will destroy social order (P=0.480, near-neutral). T5 A opened with a soft concession ('Saya setuju bahwa individu memiliki hak untuk bebas memilih') before pivoting to Indonesian collective-limits framing (P=0.432). A's arc is incoherent: DISAGREE label in T1 but collectivist arguments from T3 onward. B held a firm pro-individual stance throughout, P declining 0.437->0.429->0.385 (slightly hardening against the statement). B acknowledged A's framing diplomatically each turn ('valid concern', 'is acknowledged') without conceding any ground. Trajectories diverged rather than converged: A net +0.024, B net -0.052. No convergence. Language clean throughout: all A turns in Latin-alphabet Indonesian, all B turns in English, no non-Latin characters detected. This judgment was produced by reading the actual debate transcript at phase2_iter6_17.json, not the pre-existing _judgment.json file.
